meta.title: PTE Predictive Nomogram (3)
meta.cff_info:
PTE Predictive Nomogram (Ou et al., 2024)

Development and Validation of a Prognostic Model to Predict Late Seizures After Traumatic Brain Injury

DOI: 10.1097/SCS.0000000000010300

Factors and Points

  • Time Since Injury:
    • Each year since TBI adds 8.3 points (e.g., 5 years = 41.5 pts)
    • Max: 12 years = 100 points
  • Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) on Admission:
    • GCS < 8: 19 points
    • GCS ≥ 8: 0 points
  • Injury Location:
    • Frontal and/or Temporal lobes: 26.5 points
    • Parietal/Occipital/Other: 0 points
  • Midline Shift on Imaging:
    • Present: 33 points
    • Absent: 0 points
  • Intracranial Infection:
    • Yes: 16 points
    • No: 0 points
  • Titanium Mesh Cranioplasty:
    • Yes: 15 points
    • No: 0 points

Risk Estimates

Predicted cumulative PTE risk based on total score:

Score Risk
<500%
50–6810%
69–7720%
78–8530%
86–9440%
95–10250%
103–11160%
112–12170%
122–13780%
≥138>90%

Study Overview

Aspect Details
Population 475 patients in derivation (Nanning), 485 in validation (10 Guangxi cities)
Model Type Multivariate logistic regression
Performance AUC: 0.860 (training), 0.735 (validation)
Threshold Suggested clinical cutoff: 0.381 probability of PTE
Clinical Use Risk stratification for seizure follow-up and ASM consideration
meta.authors: Ou S, Sun L, Lu Y, et al.
meta.clinical_utility: Posttraumatic epilepsy (PTE) is considered to be one of the most severe and enduring outcomes that can arise from traumatic brain injury (TBI). The authors’ study aims to create and authenticate a prognostic model for forecasting the PTE occurrence after TBI.
meta.outcome: Development and Validation of a Prognostic Model to Predict Late Seizures After Traumatic Brain Injury: A Retrospective Analysis

predictors.name: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) on admission
predictors.assess: Select the patient’s GCS score at hospital admission. A score < 8 (19 points) indicates severe injury and contributes more to PTE risk.

predictors.name: Location
predictors.assess: Choose the primary lobar region affected by TBI. Temporal + frontal lobe injuries carry greater risk than parietal/occipital or other locations (26.5 points).

predictors.name: Midline Shift
predictors.assess: Indicate whether initial CT or MRI showed any brain midline shift. Presence of a shift reflects more severe injury and higher PTE risk (33 points).

predictors.name: Intracranial infection
predictors.assess: Select ‘Yes’ if the patient underwent decompressive craniectomy followed by titanium mesh reconstruction; this procedure is linked to elevated PTE risk (15 points).

predictors.name: Titanium mesh cranioplasty
predictors.assess: Presence of subdural hematoma increases PTE risk significantly (49 points if present).

provenance.notes: Predictors/points/rows from CFF export; complete meta from paper; verify vs live form.
